title: Buttery Silky German Chicken Stew
description: A rich and creamy German-inspired stew featuring chicken breast, bhuna spices, and a silky butter sauce. This nut-free-friendly dish is perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the bhuna curry powder, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Stir in the heavy cream and remaining 2 tablespoons of butter until the sauce is silky and smooth.
Continue to simmer for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
